verse_specific_anchors: prahasann iva — smiling as-if; the analog explicitly preserves the iva-qualifier ('not amused, but settled')
senayor ubhayor madhye — between the two armies; the analog preserves this as 'the two armies of the room'
the structural-pivot role: the explicit identification that the verse marks the moment Krishna is about to begin teaching at 2.11
verdict_reason: I tested against three candidate verses: BG 18.73 (Arjuna says 'my doubt is destroyed'), BG 11.50 (Krishna shows his gentle form again to calm Arjuna), BG 4.7-8 (Krishna names his repeated incarnations to restore dharma). None of these turn on a settled-teacher-about-to-speak posture. The analog is anchored on the unique geometry of the BG 2 chariot moment + the iva-smile + the structural-pivot-before-teaching, all of which are 2.10-specific. PASS — the analog is not transferable.
